config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikalahkan begitu saja atas nama kepentingan masyarakat, karena justru hak dasar itulah yang melindungi orang dari penyalahgunaan kuasa oleh mayoritas atau negara. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai tanpa mengorbankan kebebasan, martabat, dan perlindungan hukum tiap orang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat adalah yang menyeimbangkan keduanya, bukan menempatkan individu sebagai korban demi alasan kolektif.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From an American perspective, that kind of rule has too much potential to justify abuse by governments, majorities, or institutions, especially when they claim a "greater good" while trampling unpopular people. Society does have legitimate interests, but those should be pursued within strong limits that protect free speech, due process, bodily autonomy, and equal treatment under the law. If individual rights are treated as secondary, they stop being rights and become privileges that can be taken away whenever it is convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara otomatis di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman di Indonesia, saya justru melihat betapa mudahnya alasan “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai untuk membenarkan pembungkaman, ketidakadilan, atau penyalahgunaan kuasa terhadap orang yang lemah atau tidak populer. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar, karena tanpa perlindungan individu, mayoritas atau negara bisa bertindak semena-mena. Yang masuk akal adalah mencari keseimbangan: masyarakat dilindungi, tetapi martabat, kebebasan, dan due process tiap orang tetap menjadi batas yang tidak boleh dilanggar.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. In the United States, we have seen again and again how “the greater good” can be used to excuse censorship, discrimination, and government overreach, especially against unpopular minorities. Society absolutely has real needs, but those needs should be handled with narrow, justified limits rather than a blank check to override free speech, due process, and bodily autonomy. If rights can be set aside whenever it is convenient, then they are not really rights at all.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak keras gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Dari pengalaman di Indonesia, alasan “demi umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menutupi penyalahgunaan kuasa, membungkam yang lemah, atau mengorbankan kelompok kecil yang tidak punya suara. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi justru harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar seperti kebebasan, martabat, dan perlindungan hukum, karena tanpa itu masyarakat mudah berubah jadi alat mayoritas atau negara. Jadi menurut saya, yang benar bukan mendahulukan salah satunya secara mutlak, melainkan menjaga agar kepentingan publik tidak pernah melanggar batas hak individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support putting society’s interests ahead of individual rights as a general principle. From a United States perspective, that approach is too easy to abuse, because “the public good” has often been used to justify censorship, discrimination, and government overreach against unpopular people. Society does have legitimate needs, but they should be pursued with narrow rules that respect due process, free speech, bodily autonomy, and equal protection. To me, individual rights are not obstacles to society; they are the boundaries that keep society from becoming oppressive.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
